When we say that dirty tanker 5-yr old prices have followed resale values within normal ranges, it is not to say that they are not approaching cyclical peaks. It is the record resale values vs newbuildings ratios that are lifting 5 yrs to lofty levels.
#oott #tankers $FRO $DHT $INSW $ECO $TNK $TEN
quoted_text:
Dirty tanker 5-yr and 10-yr old prices have followed resale values within normal ranges, but the record resale values vs newbuildings ratio has placed 5-yr old prices at historical peaks vs newbuildings.
$FRO $DHT $INSW $ECO $TNK $TEN https://t.co/VC4HWvV5b0

text:
In the older age range, the dirty tanker 15-yr old premia to scrap have reached record levels for Afras and Suezmaxes. This is especially true in percent premium to scrap terms. The 15-yr old premium for VLs is not near the 2008 peaks, but remains elevated.

text:
Speculators are almost all on the same side of the boat. 

Brent net longs are near record lows while shorts hover near record highs. 

Crowded trades can persist—but they rarely end quietly.

 #Oil #OOTT #Brent #EnergyMarkets #Macro
quoted_text:
Managed money accounts continued to reduce bullish Brent crude bets in the week to 30 June, cutting the net long by 38% to near a historic low of just 55.6k contracts, down around 87% from a March peak of 429k. With the gross short hovering near an all-time high at 226k contracts, the long-short ratio has slumped to just 1.2 longs per short, also a historically low level that in the past has often proved unsustainable and triggered sharp bursts of position adjustment.

For now, however, the wave

quoted_text:
SAUDI ARABIA SLASHES OIL PRICE TO ASIA

Saudi Arabia cut the August price of its flagship Arab Light crude for Asia by $11 a barrel—the biggest reduction in at least 26 years.

The move reflects weaker oil markets, rising global supply and intensifying competition, as Gulf producers ramp up exports following the easing of disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

text:
#KSA is selling at a discount, as if there were a constant threat of sanctions.  

Iranian sanctions. 
#OOTT
quoted_text:
Saudi Arabia slashes the official selling price of its flagship Arab Light, pricing the barrels for Asia delivery at **minus** $1.5 a barrel to the Dubai benchmark.

It's the largest **discount** for Arab Light to Asia since mid-2020, when the world was still battling Covid-19.
